mechanism_narrative: Low and stable unemployment indicates a healthy labor market, supporting consumer spending and corporate profits, which translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative more than 50% of the time when UNRATE is below 4.0% over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant acceleration in retail sales indicates robust consumer demand, boosting corporate revenues and investor confidence, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite positive signal over a 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate is low and the unemployment rate is also low, it signals an accommodative monetary policy in a strong labor market, which typically supports equity valuations and leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 1.55% and UNRATE at 3.5% as of t-1mo
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 40% of periods where both FEDFUNDS < 2.0% and UNRATE < 4.0% over a 10-year period.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of strong industrial production growth, corporate earnings are expected to rise, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days. This effect is diminished or reversed during periods of contraction.
failure_condition: The correlation between INDPRO growth and SPX 5-day returns is negative or statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) during periods where INDPRO growth is positive over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A surge in housing starts indicates strong consumer and business confidence, leading to increased economic activity and higher corporate profits, which supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are negative in more than 50% of periods when HOUST is above 1300 over a 7-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Moderating inflation, as indicated by a stable or slightly declining CPI, suggests that the central bank may maintain an accommodative stance, which is generally positive for equity valuations and S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 45% of periods where CPIAUCSL 3-month change is below 0.1% over a 10-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Strong and consistent job growth, as measured by non-farm payrolls, signals a robust economy with increasing consumer purchasing power, leading to higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are negative in more than 50% of periods when PAYEMS 3-month average growth is above 150k over a 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real GDP indicates a healthy and expanding economy, which provides a strong fundamental backdrop for corporate profitability and thus drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 40% of periods when GDPC1 year-over-year growth is above 2.0% over a 10-year period.
